title: Tamago Sando

overview.description: A classic Japanese egg sandwich made with creamy egg salad between soft, fluffy milk bread.

ingredients: 4 slices soft white bread (preferably milk bread)
method: Place the eggs in a saucepan of cold water.
Bring to the boil, then cook for 9 minutes.
Transfer immediately to iced water and cool completely.
Mash two eggs thoroughly with a fork.
Roughly chop the remaining two eggs for a mixture of smooth and chunky texture.
Mix with the Japanese mayonnaise, sugar, a pinch of salt and a little white pepper.
Lightly butter the bread if using.
Spread the egg mixture generously between the slices.
Cut in half and serve immediately.

learning.did-you-know: Tamago Sando is sold everywhere in Japan, from convenience stores to specialist cafés, and is famous for its soft texture and perfectly cooked eggs.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How varying the texture of the eggs creates a more interesting filling, and why simplicity demands careful attention to detail.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick, satisfying breakfast or lunch that proves a handful of everyday ingredients can produce something genuinely special.
chapman-tips: Using both mashed and chopped eggs gives the best texture.
Cool the eggs completely before mixing with the mayonnaise.
Japanese mayonnaise gives the most authentic flavour, but homemade or good-quality regular mayonnaise also works well.
Trim the crusts just before serving so the bread stays soft.
Don't overwork the filling. A few larger pieces of egg make the sandwich much more interesting than a completely smooth paste.
notes.leftovers: Best eaten the day it is made.
